headline: US-Iran peace framework FINALIZED Sunday night: Trump posts 'deal is complete', signing Friday in Switzerland — risk premium unwinds, Brent toward $80, S&P futures +1.5%, Nikkei +5.5%, Kospi +5.7%
summary: The Iran/Hormuz overhang flagged Thursday/Friday hardened into a formal deal late Sunday. President Trump said on social media the agreement is 'now complete' with an official signing ceremony in Switzerland on Friday — a step beyond Friday's '2-3 days away' framing. Strait of Hormuz reopens, oil sanctions to lift. WTI -4.77% to $80.83/bbl; Brent toward $80. Treasury yields fell sharply (rate-cut bets re-priced), dollar softened. Risk-on globally: Japan Nikkei 225 +5.5%, South Korea Kospi +5.7%, S&P futures +1.5%. Read-through for the landscape: (1) lower oil = lower input cost for hyperscaler buildouts and grid hardware; (2) tail-risk discount in long-duration AI infra names compresses; (3) memory-chip leaders (Samsung +8.97%, SK Hynix +15.97% on the Kospi rally) capture both geopolitical relief AND structural HBM supercycle bid. Watch: deal text on Friday for any caveats on uranium enrichment, sanctions snap-back triggers, or Hormuz access guarantees.

headline: Asia memory complex EXPLODES on Iran relief + HBM bid: Samsung +8.97% to KRW 322k, SK Hynix +15.97% to KRW 2.215M (record high), Kospi +5.7%, Nikkei +5.5% — Tokyo Electron and Shin-Etsu also bid
summary: The Kospi printed an all-time high on Monday's session led by the memory complex: Samsung Electronics (005930.KS) closed +8.97% at KRW 322,000 and SK Hynix (000660.KS) closed +15.97% at KRW 2,215,000 — SK Hynix's largest single-session gain of 2026. Japan's Nikkei 225 closed +5.5% with Tokyo Electron (8035.T) and Shin-Etsu Chemical (4063.T) participating. The setup combines two reinforcing catalysts: (1) Iran peace deal completion (geopolitical risk-off → risk-on rotation into beta), and (2) the structural HBM tailwind already in place — Samsung/SK Hynix Q1 FY26 op profits of $36.1B / $27.8B respectively, 2026 HBM3E supply sold out, and TrendForce's ~20% HBM3E price-hike read-through into H2. SK Hynix has signaled US ADR listing approval is imminent. The South Korean export data printed strong overnight, reinforcing the semiconductor-led rally narrative.

headline: US AI chip complex rallies pre-market: NVDA +2%, AMD +4%, INTC +2%, AVGO higher — Daiwa upgrades Broadcom to Buy with $225 PT, citing ASIC / networking / VMware / core semi 'four strong drivers'
summary: Pre-market price action mirrors Asia's rip: Nvidia (NVDA) +2%, AMD +4%+, Intel (INTC) +2%, Broadcom (AVGO) higher — the AI infrastructure complex rebounding hard from early-June Broadcom-guide-driven drawdown. Daiwa's Louis Miscioscia upgraded AVGO to Buy from Outperform with $225 PT (cut from $275 to reflect the post-selloff base; stock still trades ~33% off its Dec 2024 high). The four-driver thesis: (1) ASIC custom silicon ramping with Google/Meta, (2) AI networking switch share gains, (3) VMware acquisition continuing to compound, (4) core semiconductor cycle. AVGO premarket print ~$168.89, +$0.37. The upgrade adds to the constructive analyst tape post-Broadcom Q3 disappointment, suggesting buy-side rotation back into AVGO at compressed multiples. Combined with the BofA INTC double-upgrade, AMD BofA-to-$560 and STM BofA-to-$100 last week, Daiwa AVGO completes a near-uniform constructive desk rotation across the AI semi complex into Micron earnings (June 24).

headline: SpaceX (SPCX) +8.1% in early Monday trading as underwriters EXERCISE OVERALLOTMENT: total raise climbs to $85.7B from initial $75B — largest IPO in history; signals deep appetite for AI/tech infrastructure paper
summary: SpaceX's IPO machine kept running into the weekend: underwriters exercised the 30-day overallotment option in full (83.3M additional Class A shares at $135 fixed), bringing total proceeds to ~$85.7B from the $75B initial raise. SPCX traded +8.1% to ~$173.95 early Monday. Implied market cap at the $173.95 print is ~$2.3T — well above the $1.77T pricing valuation, larger than every public company except a handful. Signaling read: (1) public-market appetite for AI/space infra paper is unimpaired despite June semi volatility, (2) successful full overallotment after Senator Warren's SEC delay letter (June 10) and dual-class structure complaints means risk-on capital is mobilized, (3) Nasdaq's $85B index-flow event will mechanically force passive bid into SPCX through summer — likely a marginal funding-source drag on existing megacaps via rebalance. Read-through for tracked names is muted (no direct AI infra link), but the index/liquidity dynamic matters for hyperscaler weights in particular.

headline: Micron (MU) earnings preview countdown: prints FQ3 June 24 — guide $33.5B ±$750M, GM 81%, consensus $34.8B/$19.82; HBM3E entirely sold out 2026, 2027 'mostly committed' — Goldman PT $900, BofA $675, range $249-$1,750
summary: With 7 trading days to MU's FQ3 print on June 24, the buy-side setup is binary. Company guide ($33.5B revenue ±$750M, ~81% gross margin, ~$19.15 non-GAAP EPS) sits below 44-analyst consensus ($34.8B / $19.82) — the bull case is a clean beat-and-raise driven by HBM ASP, with H2 HBM3E supply fully booked and Samsung-style multi-year customer prepayments. Goldman Sachs raised PT to $900 from $400 on June 14 (Neutral maintained) — a 125% PT hike with rating UNCHANGED, signaling the desk acknowledges the cycle but not yet conviction on entry. Wider PT range: $249 (low) to $1,750 (high), median ~$717. MU is the cleanest pure-play vehicle for the HBM supercycle if the print confirms 2026 sell-out + 2027 commitments — a miss or any guide-down on HBM ASP would re-rate the entire memory complex (which just rallied 16% on SK Hynix alone). Position-sizing risk into print is meaningful.

headline: Constellation Energy (CEG) sees analyst PT trim to ~$370 from ~$400 area; shares +4.9% to ~$316 on the week — nuclear-PPA thesis intact but valuation gap widens vs Vistra
summary: Constellation Energy saw a notable analyst fair-value cut over the weekend — consensus PT moving from ~$399.93 toward $370.58 — even as shares rallied +4.9% to $316 on the week, narrowing the implied upside. The trim is driven by: (1) repricing of the long-duration cash-flow stream after the Oracle-induced rate-volatility spike (10Y briefly +20bps before Sunday's Iran-relief drop), (2) growing recognition that Meta's January nuclear deal funded Vistra (2.176 GW Perry/Davis-Besse) plus Oklo/TerraPower — i.e. spreading nuclear-PPA capital across the field rather than concentrating in CEG. Vistra-vs-CEG comp work is consistently flagging Vistra's lower implied multiple. Thesis-altering? No — CEG's Three Mile Island restart + 20-year Microsoft Pennsylvania PPA remains the cleanest hyperscaler-nuclear pairing. But forward TR may underwhelm if 2027 capacity prices don't ramp on pace with Meta's incremental 2026 buildout demand.

headline: Empire State Manufacturing (June): General Business Conditions PRINTS 5.7 vs +13.2 consensus — softer than expected but new orders +3.5 (positive), shipments +8.6, employment +9.6 holds — Fed-cut bets unchanged
summary: The first regional Fed manufacturing print of June came in BELOW expectations: NY Fed General Business Conditions index at 5.7 vs +13.2 consensus (and vs prior). Internals were less bad than the headline: New Orders +3.5 (modest expansion), Shipments +8.6 (expansion), Employment index held steady at +9.6 — a 5th consecutive month of hiring growth in NY State factories. Inflation sub-indices remained sticky. The soft headline reinforces the post-Iran-deal narrative of falling Treasury yields and softer dollar — but does not push the Fed pivot ahead of FOMC. Industrial Production (9:15) consensus +0.2% MoM (vs +0.7% prior); Capacity Utilization consensus 76.2%. Read-through: confirms goods-side cooling already visible in May PPI tape, but the AI capex pulse (which dominates IP gains via tech/equipment) likely keeps headline IP positive. Pre-market unaffected by the print — Iran/Hormuz dynamic dominates.

headline: Meta (META) India deal: 168 MW datacenter LEASE from Reliance + 1 GW renewable energy JV with CleanMax & Fourth Partner — first major Indian hyperscale footprint, hard read for Mukesh Ambani's AI-infra play
summary: Meta confirmed last week it will LEASE a 168 MW AI-enabled datacenter from Reliance Industries in India, with Reliance delivering the facility within two years and an option to scale. In parallel, Meta inked nearly 1 GW of renewables across northern/southern Indian states with CleanMax and Fourth Partner Energy. The deal materially expands Meta's geographic capex footprint — until now its hyperscale buildout had been ~95% North America (Hyperion/Prometheus campuses) plus Europe. Implications: (1) the $115-135B Meta FY26 capex run-rate now has an India lane, (2) Reliance becomes a serious DC landlord — a structurally negative read for Equinix/Digital Realty in APAC if Reliance replicates the model with AWS/Google, (3) confirms emerging-market AI-infra demand pull is here, not just US. Renewable PPAs to India clean-power developers (vs the US nuclear-PPA path) reflects local grid reality and lower SMR availability. Updated: was originally announced June 10; including in today's brief as it remains under-discussed in tracked-name flow.
